response:
The error occurs because your WPF project is targeting the .NET Framework runtime while your main application runs on .NET 7, causing a mismatch in assembly loading when the system tries to resolve the PresentationFramework dependency on Windows.

To resolve this issue without reverting to .NET Framework, you must update your WPF project file to target net7.0-windows instead of net48 or any other .NET Framework version, ensuring it compiles as a native .NET 7 assembly that matches your main application.

cousin projects often share similar dependency structures, but in this specific case you need to explicitly add the Microsoft.NET.Sdk.WindowsDesktop package to your WPF project file to enable WPF support within the .NET 7 cross-platform build pipeline.

After updating the target framework and adding the necessary SDK package, you should also verify that your project references do not include any old .NET Framework assemblies that might force the runtime to look for the legacy version 4.0.0.0 of PresentationFramework.

Once these changes are applied and the solution is rebuilt, the WPF window will load correctly on Windows using the .NET 7 runtime while remaining excluded from the Mac build, effectively solving the file not found exception without breaking cross-platform compatibility.
